title: QUIC RFC 9000 Bootstrap Replay ACK Piggyback Work Item
summary: Implement and prove fresh pending Initial ACK prefixing for ordinary non-Retry bootstrap Initial replay probe packets without changing retained ClientHello bytes, empty token handling, or packet-number continuity.
planned_changes:
- Wire the ordinary bootstrap Initial replay helper to build a transient Initial ACK frame payload when Initial ACK-generation state has pending ack-eliciting receipts.
- Pass that transient ACK payload to the existing Initial packet builder before the replayed CRYPTO frame while preserving the original Initial destination connection ID and empty token.
- Mark the piggybacked Initial ACK frame as sent only after the replay packet is tracked under its actual new Initial packet number.
- Preserve existing behavior when no Initial ACK is pending.
- Keep Retry-selected replay, delayed-ACK scheduling, path scheduling, interop success, and public API behavior outside this slice.
- Add focused positive, negative, deterministic fuzz/property, and BenchmarkDotNet coverage for the ordinary bootstrap replay packet-builder path.
verification_plan: Run the focused RFC 9000 S13P2P1-0010 requirement-home tests, run adjacent RFC 9002 bootstrap Initial PTO replay tests, run the long-header packet-protection benchmark suite with BenchmarkDotNet Dry and Short jobs, render/check the touched SpecTrace artifacts, validate JSON and preserve standing failures if present, run `git diff --check`, and preserve evidence under `artifacts/verification/20260425-rfc9000-bootstrap-replay-ack-piggyback/` and `artifacts/benchmark-baseline/20260425-rfc9000-bootstrap-replay-ack-piggyback/`.
out_of_scope: Retry-selected Initial replay ACK piggybacking already owned by WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0022.
Application Data stream, NEW_TOKEN, live long-header CRYPTO, and CRYPTO retransmission ACK piggybacking already owned by earlier slices.
completion_notes: Completed as a bounded ordinary non-Retry bootstrap Initial replay packet-composition floor. Broader delayed-ACK scheduling, path scheduling, interop, and public API behavior remain outside this work item.
